title: remove switchboard stdev
author: jgur-psyops
body: switchboard prices no longer use the stdev field (it's always zero), usage of this can be removed and/or replaced by some fixed or per-bank configurable confidence interval.

title: Lending events missing share_amount — indexers cannot reconstruct share-based balances
author: LixvYang
body:
The four core lending events (`LendingAccountDepositEvent`, `LendingAccountWithdrawEvent`, `LendingAccountBorrowEvent`, `LendingAccountRepayEvent`) only emit the raw token `amount: u64`. They do not include a corresponding share delta field.

This means indexers and off-chain services cannot accurately reconstruct a user's share-based balance from event history without either:
- Re-deriving the bank's cumulative asset/liability share price at every block (requires oracle prices + interest accrual), or
- Continuously polling on-chain account state (misses intermediate positions).

### Affected event structs (`programs/marginfi/src/events.rs:141-172`)

```rust
pub struct LendingAccountDepositEvent {
    pub header: AccountEventHeader,
    pub bank: Pubkey,
    pub mint: Pubkey,
    pub amount: u64,          // token amount only
}

pub struct LendingAccountWithdrawEvent {
    pub header: AccountEventHeader,
    pub bank: Pubkey,
    pub mint: Pubkey,
    pub amount: u64,          // token amount only
    pub close_balance: bool,
}

pub struct LendingAccountBorrowEvent {
    pub header: AccountEventHeader,
    pub bank: Pubkey,
    pub mint: Pubkey,
    pub amount: u64,          // token amount only
}

pub struct LendingAccountRepayEvent {
    pub header: AccountEventHeader,
    pub bank: Pubkey,
    pub mint: Pubkey,
    pub amount: u64,          // token amount only
    pub close_balance: bool,
}
```

### Where share values are computed but not emitted

- **deposit.rs** — `bank_account.deposit()` → `increase_balance_internal()` computes asset share delta via `bank.get_asset_shares(amount)`
- **withdraw.rs** — `bank_account.withdraw()` → `decrease_balance_internal()` computes asset share delta
- **borrow.rs** — `bank_account.borrow()` → `decrease_balance_internal()` computes liability share delta via `bank.get_liability_shares()`
- **repay.rs** — `bank_account.repay()` → `increase_balance_internal()` computes liability share delta via `bank.get_liability_shares()`

### Reference

The test reference implementation at `tests/refs/programs/lending/src/events.rs` already includes share fields:

```rust
pub struct LogDeposit {
    pub assets: u64,
    pub shares_minted: u64,
}

pub struct LogWithdraw {
    pub assets: u64,
    pub shares_burned: u64,
}
```

### Suggested fix

Add a `share_amount: u64` field to each of the four events, and populate it at each emit site with the share delta already computed in the corresponding `increase_balance_internal` / `decrease_balance_internal` calls.

title: Timelocked-admin for most general admin functions
author: jgur-psyops
body:
In light of the Drift exploit, we want to move some operations to a timelocked admin.

We want to remove the group admin's ability to do the following, while enabling a new  "timelocked_admin" to do them. The new timelocked admin is also the only one who can change the timelocked admin. These tasks, considered "high risk", 

should include:
* adding banks (including all integrations)
* configuring bank oracles (including fixed price)
* removing a bank from reduce/paused mode to operational
* switching a bank from isolated to collateral
* anything else where an attacker might edit financial info in such a way that could cause a serious loss

should not include:
* Pausing the protocol
* Adjusting deposit/withdraw caps
* Adjusting interest curves
* Setting a bank to reduce mode

title: Pause delegate admin
author: jgur-psyops
body:
Reminder to contributors: Please base off the latest version branch, and target the consolidated update with your PR, not main. The main branch reflects the latest version on mainnet, and may be 1-2 months stale compared to what's in active development. Right now, that's 0.1.9-main, and https://github.com/mrgnlabs/marginfi-v2/pull/538


### Summary

The protocol is currently paused/unpaused by the global admin, which requires the primary MS to sign. We keep a pause tx pending to execute to allow us to pause quickly as-needed, but if often gets cancelled as we conduct membership changes in the MS, so we wish to create a delegate admin for pause functionality. A lower traffic and lower threshold MS will hold this admin, which is only able to pause/unpause the program.

title: Discovery: On-chain compression for inactive MarginfiAccounts (recoverable rent)
author: ZygmuntJakub
body:
Hey! I ran a full scan of Marginfi on mainnet and benchmarked on-chain LZ4 compression for inactive `MarginfiAccount` accounts. Numbers came out interesting, sharing in case the team finds it useful.

**Mainnet scan** ([script](https://github.com/ZygmuntJakub/densol/blob/main/benchmark/marginfi/scan.ts), full scan of all 512,497 accounts):

| Status | Count | % |
|---|---|---|
| Inactive (no tx > 30 d) | **506,233** | 98.8% |
| Active | 6,264 | 1.2% |

**On-chain compression benchmark** ([script](https://github.com/ZygmuntJakub/densol/blob/main/benchmark/marginfi/benchmark.ts), 50 random inactive accounts):

```
SOL price: $81.76 (Binance)

Sampled 50 accounts from 506,233 inactive
Account size from scan: 2312 B

  #  Pubkey                RawSz   CompSz   Ratio     CompCU   DecompCU   RentSaved
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  1  V5FYj9…aZA3kT        2312 B    121 B   19.1x     26,200     37,599  0.0152 SOL
...
 50  DscQhn…xSwUdN        2312 B    113 B   20.5x     25,810     37,501  0.0153 SOL

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Raw size:          2312 B avg  (min 2312 B, max 2312 B)
Compressed size:   140 B avg  (min 113 B, max 293 B)
Compression ratio: 17.85x avg
Compress CU:       26,751  (one-time keeper cost)
Decompress CU:     37,425  (per chunk read, on demand)
Rent saved:        0.015114 SOL/account
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Extrapolation — all inactive Marginfi MarginfiAccounts on mainnet
  (source: benchmark/marginfi/scan.ts)
  inactive accounts: 506,233
  recoverable rent:  ~7651 SOL  (~$625553 at $81.76/SOL)
```

**How it works:** [densol](https://crates.io/crates/densol) is a `no_std` LZ4 library that runs inside the Solana SBF VM, no libc, no std allocator, fits within the 32 KB heap. At 2,312 B, `MarginfiAccount` compresses in a single `ChunkedLZ4` pass at 26,714 CU.

Since `MarginfiAccount` is `zero_copy` (`bytemuck::Pod`), integration would use a hibernation pattern, flag in `account_flags` marks the account as compressed, the compressed bytes occupy the shrunken account, and on first transaction the program decompresses and restores the account.

**For curiosity, has this been considered ever?**

title: config emits op mode
author: jgur-psyops
body:
Reminder to contributors: Please base off the latest version branch, and target the consolidated update with your PR, not main. The main branch reflects the latest version on mainnet, and may be 1-2 months stale compared to what's in active developement. Right now, that's 0.1.8-main, and https://github.com/mrgnlabs/marginfi-v2/pull/493


# Summary

Configuring a bank's operational mode is currently silent, it doesn't emit a log. Emit some simple log (e.g. Op mode: r/o/p) when a bank's operational mode is adjusted in the config instruction. Likewise for other risk tier.

title: configure_group take optional keys
author: jgur-psyops
body:
Reminder to contributors: Please base off the latest version branch, and target the consolidated update with your PR, not main. The main branch reflects the latest version on mainnet, and may be 1-2 months stale compared to what's in active developement. Right now, that's 0.1.8-main, and https://github.com/mrgnlabs/marginfi-v2/pull/493


# Summary

Since the configure group instruction now sets several different admins, it should take a Option<Pubkey> for each admin instead of a PubKey directly, so the caller can opt to skip setting up whichever admin they are not interested in, reduce the odds of sausage fingers settings the wrong admin or restoring some old admin.

title: Risk-admin only close LiquidationRecord
author: jgur-psyops
body:
Reminder to contributors: Please base off the latest version branch, and target the consolidated update with your PR, not main. The main branch reflects the latest version on mainnet, and may be 1-2 months stale compared to what's in active developement. Right now, that's 0.1.8-main, and https://github.com/mrgnlabs/marginfi-v2/pull/493

# Summary

There is currently no way to recover rent from the LiquidationRecord, which is somewhat by design since it serves as a permanent record of receivership liquidations, but on a week like this one, we end up creating quite a lot of them, which really adds up!

The main blocker to closing LiquidationRecords has been coming up with a good criteria. For now, create a risk-admin only instruction to close LiquidationRecords, so we can recover rent from deleveraged or near-empty accounts. Creating new records is simply, so this is relatively low risk.

title: Same-asset automatic emode
author: jgur-psyops
body:
Reminder to contributors: Please base off the latest version branch, and target the consolidated update with your PR, not main. The main branch reflects the latest version on mainnet, and may be 1-2 months stale compared to what's in active developement. Right now, that's 0.1.8-main, and https://github.com/mrgnlabs/marginfi-v2/pull/493

# Summary 

When two banks have exactly the same mint, there is no reason not to enable near-infinite leverage, since it’s riskless for them to be under (even to the point of “bad debt”), unless there is some kind of hack/haircut/socialization event. Same-mint banks should be “automatically” eligible for emode at some arbitrary very high leverage (configurable at the group level would be nice). For example, Kamino USDC should have very high leverage against P0 USDC. 

There might some interplay between cToken exchange rates that makes this tricky at very high leverages, so tests should validate (including via health pulse) very conclusively at even at e.g. 100x leverage we are never offering under-collat lending.

This enables simple basis-trade like lend Kamino USDC borrow P0 USDC, which creates incredible (if often brief) arb opportunities to earn risk-free 3 figure interest rates.

# Summary 

Partially blocks #485 until completed

In order to support borrow/withdraw windows, we must force the caller to pass risk accounts for all balances, including the one being withdraw/repaid in withdraw-all and repay-all paths. Currently, we let the caller omit those.

title: Daily withdraw/borrow limit
author: jgur-psyops
body:
Reminder to contributors: Please base off the latest version branch, and target the consolidated update with your PR, not main. The main branch reflects the latest version on mainnet, and may be 1-2 months stale compared to what's in active developement. Right now, that's 0.1.8-main, and https://github.com/mrgnlabs/marginfi-v2/pull/493

# Summary 

We want to impose a reasonable per-bank limit on daily withdraws/borrows. It suffices to impose a 24-hour limit that reset to zero at some fixed time each day, though a sliding-24-hour window would be cooler.

* Denominated in native token
* Permission-less reset (admin doesn't have to do anything to reset the window)
* Should exclude flashloan, liquidation, and deleverage traffic - all other kinda of financial txes increment/decrement the window and will fail when it's exceeded until it resets
* Deposits offset withdraws, same with repays/borrows, so spamming deposit/withdraw doesn't consume any of the window

Nice to haves:
* global (per group) windows on a dollar basis, e.g. $10M in withdraws/day
* Hourly and Daily windows, with different limits

title: Fixed prices with kamino/drift/etc
author: jgur-psyops
body:
Reminder to contributors: Please base off the latest version branch, and target the consolidated update with your PR, not main. The main branch reflects the latest version on mainnet, and may be 1-2 months stale compared to what's in active developement. Right now, that's 0.1.8-main, and https://github.com/mrgnlabs/marginfi-v2/pull/493

# Summary 

While the fixed-price feature "works" for Kamino/Drift/etc, it doesn't account for interest that accumulates on those banks, which makes it impractical. We should have a fixed-price oracle type for those asset types that (1) fixes the "base" price that we normally get from Pyth/Swb/etc, (2) continues to multiply by the exchange rate for that venue's underlying Reserve/Market/etc.

title: Same-bank rewards system
author: jgur-psyops
body:
Reminder to contributors: Please base off the latest version branch, and target the consolidated update with your PR, not main. The main branch reflects the latest version on mainnet, and may be 1-2 months stale compared to what's in active developement. Right now, that's 0.1.8-main, and https://github.com/mrgnlabs/marginfi-v2/pull/493

# Summary 

When users earn an emission/incentive in the same token as the bank, we should have an instruction to simply admin-deposit this to the bank’s liquidity pool and increase asset share value, automatically benefiting all depositors. 

We can call this something like "emissions_deposit" and it could be permissionless.

If the incentive is more complex, e.g. in a different token than the bank's token, we'll continue to deal with those OTC for now.

Drift has a similar mechanism that can act as a reference.

title: ratepoints human-readable in add/configure bank
author: jgur-psyops
body: Currently rate points output in their raw u32 form, which is not human-readable without conversion. Display the f32 or f64 "actual" rate for easier tx preview.

title: Stale blockhash for rust tests
author: abelmarnk
body:
### Problem
The rust tests currently use `ProgramTestContext.last_blockhash` as the blockhash that is passed to each transaction sent in the tests, but this may sometimes cause intermittent blockhash errors when there is a delay in the tests, this is because that blockhash is only updated on [initialization](https://github.com/anza-xyz/agave/blob/e5ec8630e2074984f0eca4934aefea507eaa2e17/program-test/src/lib.rs#L1087), or when explicitly [jumping to a new slot](https://github.com/anza-xyz/agave/blob/e5ec8630e2074984f0eca4934aefea507eaa2e17/program-test/src/lib.rs#L1235), or when it is explicitly [refreshed](https://github.com/anza-xyz/agave/blob/e5ec8630e2074984f0eca4934aefea507eaa2e17/program-test/src/lib.rs#L1290) or through some other explicit means.

### Possible solution
Replace `ProgramTestContext.last_blockhash` with `ProgramTestContext.get_new_latest_blockhash` or some other retry method.

title: Investigate doing kamino refreshes inside the program
author: Henry-E
body:
Reminder to contributors: Please base off the latest version branch, and target the consolidated update with your PR, not main. The main branch reflects the latest version on mainnet, and may be 1-2 months stale compared to what's in active developement. Right now, that's 0.1.8-main, and https://github.com/mrgnlabs/marginfi-v2/pull/493

# Summary 

Initially the idea was to leave the refreshes of the obligation and reserves as something for whomever is constructing the transaction but it seems like this has caused some issues with transaction sizes. So it's worth revisiting and evaluating what the tradeoffs and requirements are for automatically including the refresh instructions as CPIs.
